channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Call it the Dog Bowl. Westminster show's canine athletes get their piece of Super Bowl weekend
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — They're at the top of their sport. They run, weave and go airborne. And they're going all out to win the championship this weekend. Sorry — no, they're not the Chiefs or the Eagles.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Missing Alaska flight found with no survivors: What to know
item.description: The plane, operated by Bering Air, had been traveling from Unalakleet to Nome when it vanished on Thursday, only to be found the next day.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tips to easily cancel your subscriptions
item.description: The FTC says that it received about 70 consumer complaints a day in 2024 about recurring subscriptions, up from 42 per day in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, NewsNation

item.title: 2 people charged in fatal stabbing of Fort Campbell soldier in Tennessee, police say
item.description: CLARKSVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Two people have been charged in the death of a Fort Campbell soldier who was stabbed nearly 70 times last year, police said Saturday. Sofia Rodas, 35, has been charged with first-degree murder and tampering with evidence in the death of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 million donut products recalled nationwide over listeria risk: FDA
item.description: More than 2 million cases of baked goods distributed throughout the nation are being recalled this year due to the possible presence of listeria monocytogenes.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, ABC 4

item.title: Dozens more Alexander brother accusers come forward as real estate tycoons plead not guilty
item.description: Luxury real estate brokers Tal and Oren Alexander and their brother Alon pleaded not guilty to sex trafficking charges, and prosecutors say at least 60 victims have come forward.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Family files lawsuit after mother, son die from food poisoning while on vacation
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says some white South Africans are oppressed, could be resettled in the US. They say no thanks
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — Groups representing some of South Africa's white minority responded Saturday to a plan by President Donald Trump to offer them refugee status and resettlement in the United States by saying: thanks, but no thanks.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Leaders from eastern and southern Africa call for a ceasefire and negotiations in Congo
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — Leaders from eastern and southern Africa on Saturday called for an immediate ceasefire in eastern Congo, where rebels are threatening to overthrow the Congolese government, but also urged Congo's president to directly negotiate with them.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A tax credit worth up to $7,830 often goes unclaimed: See who qualifies for the EITC
item.description: The IRS estimates that a fifth of eligible taxpayers fail to take advantage of the tax break each year.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Is PSN still down? PlayStation Network addressing ongoing outage
item.description: Thousands of users have reported outages with the PlayStation Network since Friday evening but as of Saturday morning things still didn't seem back normal.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KRON4

item.title: FDA recalls 2 million donut products over listeria danger
item.description: More than 2 million cases of baked goods distributed are being recalled this year due to the possible presence of listeria monocytogenes.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio city files federal lawsuit against alleged neo-Nazi group
item.description: The city of Springfield, Ohio has filed a lawsuit against an alleged neo-Nazi group for conducting what it claims to be a campaign of threats and intimidation.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, Fox 5

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: Authorities are working to recover the wreckage of a plane crash in western Alaska that killed 10 people while investigators are trying to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk perched behind Trump's desk in new TIME Magazine cover
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk is seen perched behind President Donald Trump's desk in the Oval Office in their latest cover.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KRON4

item.title: Trump's 3rd week saw more executive orders, a trade war that wasn't and a Mideast jolt
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Three weeks in, President Donald Trump keeps cranking out executive orders designed to remake the government while billionaire Elon Musk hunts for more ways to upend the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: BY WILL WEISSERT and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Here's how to watch the Super Bowl for free
item.description: The Kansas City Chiefs and Philadelphia Eagles will face off in Super Bowl LIX, two years after their matchup in Super Bowl LVII, which the Chiefs won 38-35.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-Israel protesters swarm and vandalize home of Jewish media super agent, UC regent
item.description: The home of media agent Jay Sures was vandalized by a group of anti-Israel protesters on Wednesday, who plastered his garage doors with red handprints and strung yellow caution tape throughout his front yard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs order to establish White House Faith Office
item.description: Trump directed the office to identify ways to reduce burdens on the free exercise of religion and direct all federal agencies to designate a “faith liaison” within 90 days.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KTLA 5

item.title: Democratic AGs sue over DOGE access to Treasury payment systems
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law,” James said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KTLA 5

item.title: Federal judge blocks Elon Musk's DOGE from accessing sensitive US Treasury Department material
item.description: A federal judge early Saturday blocked Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Treasury Department records that contain sensitive personal data such as Social Security and bank account numbers for millions of Americans. U.S. District Judge Paul A.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Crews rush to recover commuter plane found crashed on Alaska sea ice before expected snow and wind
item.description: Authorities are racing to recover the remains of 10 people killed in a plane crash in Western Alaska before a winter storm hits the region.
item.dc:creator: Becky Bohrer And Hallie Golden, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge blocks Trump from placing thousands of USAID workers on leave and giving them 30-day deadline
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday dealt President Donald Trump and billionaire ally Elon Musk their first big setback in their dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development, ordering a temporary halt to plans to pull thousands of agency staffers off the job. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump is signing up local law officers to help with immigration enforcement
item.description: For years, the Hamilton County Sheriff's Office in suburban Indianapolis has wanted to partner with federal immigration authorities to identify and detain immigrants who are in the U.S. illegally and facing charges.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A 15-year-old went to a Brooklyn parade. The NYPD wrongly accused him of a mass shooting
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Camden Lee was leaving high school football practice in September when he saw the photograph, splashed across the New York Police Department’s social media accounts, that would soon upend his life.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bryan Kohberger case: Expert explains how a firing squad works
item.description: "It's very rapid. You're completely unconscious very quickly," said firing squad expert Dr. James Williams.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Generations later, a remedy to destroying Black neighborhoods is fulfilled in Michigan
item.description: HAMTRAMCK, Mich. (AP) — Leslie Knox was a young girl in the 1960s when her Detroit-area city was accused of destroying neighborhoods to get rid of Black residents.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A hidden gem of whitewater rafting faces an uncertain future after Hurricane Helene
item.description: ERWIN, Tenn. (AP) — After 24 years of guiding whitewater trips on the Nolichucky River Gorge for other companies, Patrick Mannion finally received a permit last year to operate his own outfitter business.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's DEI order leaves academic researchers fearful of political influence over grants
item.description: President Donald Trump’s order declaring diversity, equity and inclusion policies illegal has thrown into doubt the future of research Kendra Dahmer has been doing on intestinal parasites in India and Benin.
item.dc:creator: Olga R. Rodriguez, Terry Chea And Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Georgia police officer shot, killed while responding to shopping center
item.description: A Georgia police officer was shot and killed Friday night when responding to a report of a suspicious person at a shopping center in the City of Roswell.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: T-Swift fan, Philly agency trolling each other ahead of Super Bowl LIX
item.description: A Taylor Swift fan from Boston and a Philadelphia-based advertising agency have spent the week leading up to Super Bowl LIX trolling each other with billboards in KC and Philly.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, Fox 5

item.title: Missing commuter plane found crashed on Alaska sea ice and all 10 aboard are dead, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — A small commuter plane that crashed in western Alaska on its way to the hub community of Nome was located Friday on sea ice, and all 10 people on board were dead, authorities said. The crash was one of the deadliest in the state in the last 25 years.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A mother and daughter were killed in tornadoes that struck eastern Tennessee
item.description: WARTBURG, Tenn. (AP) — Two tornadoes moved through eastern Tennessee, leaving a mother and daughter dead and injuring three other people, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump suggests displaced Palestinians in Gaza be 'permanently' resettled outside war-torn territory
item.description: The renewed call by the Trump administration on Arab nations to relocate displaced Palestinians comes as Trump is set to meet with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the White House later Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
